scope_note: QA Rational Surveying cert [310]. Primary sources: Iverson, B. (1975-1996) Quantum Arithmetic vols 1-2 (QA-1, QA-2); Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions: Rational Trigonometry, Wild Egg Books, ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8; Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5, Ch.XIII. QA mapping: every integer-sided right triangle is generated by a QA base pair (b,e) via the squaring map (unbounded integers, no modular reduction): d=b+e, a=b+2e, C=2de (short leg), F=d^2-e^2=ba (long leg), G=d^2+e^2 (hypotenuse, Gaussian norm), L=de*(d^2-e^2)/6 (cubic identity). This is Euclid's parametrization in QA variables; QA is the generator algebra for all Pythagorean triples. Wildberger spread replaces angle at the observer layer: arctan(F/C) is float (observer projection); F^2/G^2 is exact Fraction (QA layer). Theorem NT: land-surveying bearings are observer projections only; discrete (b,e) pairs are the QA substrate. CLAIM: (C1) C^2+F^2=G^2 for all (b,e) in {1..24}^2 (576 pairs, exact integer). (C2) Area=6L: right-triangle area C*F/2=6L exactly (C*F=12L); triangular parcel area = 6 times the cubic QA identity L. (C3) Primitivity: gcd(C,F,G)=1 iff gcd(b,e)=1 AND b is odd; equivalently iff gcd(d,e)=1 AND d,e have different parities (Euclid's theorem in QA variables). (C4) Rational spreads: s_P=F^2/G^2 and s_Q=C^2/G^2 are exact Fractions in (0,1) with s_P+s_Q=1; s_R=1 at right angle; no pi or arcsin in QA layer. (C5) Theorem NT: bearing arctan(F/C) is transcendental (float), replaced by spread F^2/G^2 (Fraction) at observer layer; bearing never re-enters QA integer logic. Builds on cert [305] (Wildberger spread in QA), cert [299] (Cassini/Fibonacci structure). Lineage: derived 2026-06-03 from Iverson QA-1/QA-2 squaring map + Wildberger rational trig.
